assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_006",
  "rule_id": "Enable FIPS Mode",
  "category": "too_dangerous_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.97,
  "rationale": "Enabling FIPS mode requires kernel boot parameter changes and cryptographic policy updates that can affect system boot and cryptographic operations. Applying this on an existing system is unsupported and may render the system unbootable or break services, posing a high risk.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "bootloader configuration change",
    "kernel parameter modification",
    "cryptographic policy alteration"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Verify hardware and software compatibility with FIPS",
    "Plan remediation during scheduled maintenance window",
    "Test changes in a non‑production environment before applying to production"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": true,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
